nodes.label: ui frontend react vite
nodes.description: ui/frontend/ — React + Vite single-page app. Three columns: search/type-filter/node-list sidebar (left), force-directed graph canvas (center), node detail panel (right). Header chips show live total counts. An Ingest panel modal launches new project ingestions. State is local-component; no global store — props and useCallback memos suffice at the current graph size.

nodes.label: app jsx root component
nodes.description: App.jsx — top-level component. Owns: stats, full-graph data, types list, search string, type filter, search results, selected node id, expanded node ids, error, showIngest. Fetches /api/stats, /api/types, /api/graph on mount; /api/search whenever search or typeFilter changes (debounce-free, relies on React batching). All visibility logic for the graph lives in this file via the visibleNodeIds memo.

nodes.label: importance threshold visibility
nodes.description: Constant IMPORTANCE_THRESHOLD = 0.9 in App.jsx. Default view shows only nodes with importance ≥ 0.9 — keeps the canvas readable on graphs of hundreds of nodes. The user expands explicitly (double-click) to bring in less-important neighbors. Falls back to top MIN_VISIBLE (20) nodes if the calibration is off and too few cross the threshold.

nodes.label: min visible fallback
nodes.description: MIN_VISIBLE = 20 in App.jsx. If the importance-threshold filter leaves fewer than 20 visible nodes, the top 20 by importance are added regardless. Prevents a 'blank canvas' state on a freshly-ingested graph where no node has yet been audited up to 0.9.

nodes.label: expanded node ids set
nodes.description: Set<string> in App state, populated by double-clicking a node. For each id in the set, the visibleNodeIds memo adds the node itself plus its 1-hop neighbors (via the precomputed neighborMap). Enables progressive exploration without re-fetching the graph: the graph is loaded once, the visible subset is a client-side projection.

nodes.label: neighbor map memo
nodes.description: useMemo in App.jsx building an UNDIRECTED Map<id, Set<id>> from graphData.edges. Rebuilt only when graphData changes (not on every render). Used by the visibleNodeIds memo to compute 1-hop expansions in O(1) per expanded node. The undirected view is the right one for 'show me what surrounds this node' regardless of edge direction.

nodes.label: visible node ids memo
nodes.description: useMemo computing the set of node IDs to render. Logic: if there is a search result, base = the search hits; else base = nodes above importance threshold (with MIN_VISIBLE fallback). Then for every id in expandedNodeIds, add it and its 1-hop neighbors. The graph canvas and the sidebar list both read this set, ensuring a single source of truth for 'what is currently visible'.

nodes.label: search takes over visibility
nodes.description: When the search box is non-empty, the visible node set switches from the importance-based default to the search-result set. The graph canvas re-lays-out the matching subgraph. Rationale: a user searching has a focused intent; the canvas should reflect it instantly rather than highlighting a few nodes inside a noisy whole.

nodes.label: automatic select brings into view
nodes.description: handleNodeSelect: when the user clicks an id that is NOT currently visible (e.g. from a search result outside the threshold filter), the id is added to expandedNodeIds so the canvas brings it (and its neighbors) into view automatically. Avoids the frustration of seeing a node selected in the detail panel but invisible on the canvas.

nodes.label: graph canvas react flow
nodes.description: GraphCanvas.jsx — uses @xyflow/react for rendering and interaction (drag, zoom, minimap), with a custom BrainNode node type. Node size is a function of importance (16 + importance*28 px). Edge labels are the rel type, mid-edge. Dark color mode, dotted background. Wraps the Flow component in ReactFlowProvider so the useReactFlow hook works inside.

nodes.label: d3 force layout
nodes.description: computeLayout in GraphCanvas.jsx runs a synchronous d3-force simulation (link distance 140 strength 0.4, charge -250, center, collide radius 35) for 400 ticks, then HANDS the positions to React Flow. d3-force is used purely as a layout engine — it does not animate. React Flow handles the interactive rendering. This split lets the graph settle into a readable shape before any frame is painted.

nodes.label: positions ref persistence
nodes.description: positionsRef = useRef({}) seeds the next layout with current positions. When the visible set changes (search, expand), new nodes get random initial positions but existing nodes keep their previous ones, then the force sim relaxes the whole layout. Result: existing nodes don't 'jump' when neighbors are revealed — only the new ones slide into place.

nodes.label: brain node custom component
nodes.description: Custom React Flow node type rendering a colored circle (size by importance, color by type), a truncated label below (16 chars + ellipsis), and an optional `+N` badge when the node has N hidden neighbors. Four invisible handles at top/left/bottom/right let edges connect from any side without forcing direction-specific source/target ports.

nodes.label: type color palette
nodes.description: TYPE_COLORS map: concept #4c9be8 blue, entity #52c97c green, algorithm #a78bfa violet, artifact #f59e0b orange, mechanism #ef4444 red, property #f472b6 pink, event #94a3b8 slate, claim #fb923c amber, person #22d3ee cyan, place #a3e635 lime, process #34d399 mint. Unknown types fall back to #888 grey. The same hex values are mirrored in CSS variables (--type-*) for consistency between canvas and sidebar/header chips.

nodes.label: hidden neighbor badge
nodes.description: Per-node counter computed in the Flow useEffect: for each visible node, count how many of its edges have endpoints OUTSIDE the visible set. Displayed as a `+N` badge with a tooltip ('N hidden neighbor(s) — double-click to expand'). Discoverability hint for the expand-on-double-click affordance.

nodes.label: double click to expand
nodes.description: onNodeDoubleClick → onExpandNode(id) adds the id to expandedNodeIds in App state. Triggers the recomputation of visibleNodeIds and a re-layout that pulls the 1-hop neighbors into view. Pairs with the +N badge so users discover the gesture from the visual hint.

nodes.label: reset view button
nodes.description: Visible only when expandedNodeIds is non-empty. Clears the set in one click. Lets the user undo a deep exploration without having to reload the page or pan back to the importance-default view.

nodes.label: node panel detail view
nodes.description: NodePanel.jsx — right-column detail panel. Fetches /api/node/{id} when nodeId changes. Renders header (label, type chip, importance, id), description, sources chips, Outgoing rels (rel_type, navigable target, optional factor, first evidence, confidence), Incoming rels (same structure, navigable source). Clicking a target or source label calls onNavigate(other_id) which triggers selection on the App side.

nodes.label: click to navigate neighbors
nodes.description: In NodePanel, every neighbor label is clickable: onClick calls onNavigate(neighbor_id) which propagates to App.handleNodeSelect, which selects the node AND auto-expands it into view if hidden. Enables walking the graph in a 'follow links' style without using the canvas at all.

nodes.label: ingest panel modal
nodes.description: IngestPanel.jsx — modal overlay launched from the header button. Form fields project_name (pattern [a-zA-Z0-9_-]+) and folder_path (absolute path). On submit, POSTs /api/ingest, gets a job_id, then polls /api/ingest/{job_id} every 2 seconds. Streams stdout into a <pre> that auto-scrolls. Refresh-graph button on completion triggers a fresh /api/graph and /api/stats fetch.

nodes.label: ingest polling 2s interval
nodes.description: setInterval(2000) drives the status poll while a job is running. Cleared as soon as status leaves 'running' (or on component unmount). 2 seconds is a cheap balance: feels live without spamming the backend; aligns with how slowly the Claude subprocess produces stdout lines.

nodes.label: ingest panel modal close safety
nodes.description: The overlay onClick handler only closes the modal if (a) the click was on the backdrop, not the panel, AND (b) the job is NOT currently running. Prevents accidentally dismissing a long-running ingestion. Cancel/X buttons are also hidden while running.

nodes.label: strict mode double render
nodes.description: main.jsx wraps App in React.StrictMode. Components mount-effect twice in dev; the codebase tolerates this because all fetch effects are idempotent and have no cleanup-required side effects (no subscriptions to long-lived resources at mount).

nodes.label: single source visibility truth
nodes.description: Only visibleNodeIds (computed in App.jsx) decides what is shown. The sidebar list, the canvas, and the +N badge calculation all read this set. Prevents the canvas-and-list desync that would happen if each consumer applied its own filter.

rels.evidence: Frontend fetches all data from the FastAPI backend on :8000.
